captions.02.png: Both sides of this back porch wall required  wood restorer and filler sculpted to match the original woodwork
captions.03.png: The completed section of the wall after painting.
captions.04.png: The front of the house needed scraping, sanding, and repainting. The original paint was peeling and the windows needed recaulking.
captions.05.png: The completed front of the house after painting.
captions.06.jpeg: This Hyde Park home needed a new bathroom window for ventilation and code compliance. The walls of these old houses were built with studs that are actually 2 inchs by 4 inches so the inside required some custom trim work to tie into the shower stall.
captions.07.jpeg: The original trim was preserved and the owner chose to paint it himself afterwards
captions.08.jpeg: The original back door was drafty and the owner wanted to replace it with a more energy efficient door that he found at Habitat for Humanity.
captions.09.jpeg: This back wall wasn't remotely plumb but the door sure needed to be. I used a trick i learned form an old home builder and cut some long thin triangles out 1 x 4s to accommodate for the gaps between the jamb and the trim. you can see some this in the next photo on the right side of the door.
captions.10.jpeg: I also filled this gap in the floor with foamand reused part of the original threshold to maintain the look. The owners were very happy with the results and their dog appreciates the new view out the back door.
